narrative: As a white male applicant with an upper-middle-class family background and plans to attend law school full-time, your profile stood out in some positive ways, but the admissions model focused heavily on predictors of bar exam success. The model assessed your overall chances and predicted a 95.9% probability that you would fail the bar exam on your first try, which is why your application was rejected—law schools use this prediction to prioritize students likely to pass and succeed in practice. The three biggest factors driving this tough prediction were your Year 3 school decile ranking, your LSAT score, and your Year 1 school decile ranking, in that order of influence. Your Year 3 decile of 1—far below the typical student's 5.5—had the strongest pull toward failure, as it signals consistently low performance relative to peers that often foreshadows bar struggles. Similarly, your LSAT score of 26, compared to the average of about 37, reinforced this risk by showing a gap in the standardized reasoning skills vital for the exam. Even your Year 1 decile of 1, well under the average of nearly 6, added to the concern, indicating early academic challenges that carried through. While your undergraduate GPA of 2.8 is a bit below average and your full-time status aligns with most students, these played smaller roles here. In contrast to typical admitted students, who often rank in the middle or higher deciles and score much stronger on the LSAT, your metrics placed you in a high-risk group for first-time bar passage. This isn't a judgment on your potential or background—many factors like family income didn't weigh heavily against you—but the model prioritizes these academic signals based on patterns from past students. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT to boost your score, strengthening your recent academic record through additional coursework or a post-bac program, and reapplying after demonstrating improvement. You might also explore part-time programs or schools with stronger support for bar prep, tailoring your next application to highlight any upward trends. With targeted effort, you can shift these key factors and improve your odds significantly.
